paper_map.title: Lightweight Fe–Mn–Al–C Steels: Current State, Manufacturing, and Implementation Prospects
paper_map.abstract_summary: This review examines the effect of alloying elements on the microstructure and mechanical properties of austenitic Fe–Mn–Al–C steels, with a focus on κ-carbide formation and distribution. It discusses manufacturing processes, strengthening mechanisms (TRIP, TWIP, MBIP) governed by stacking fault energy, and challenges hindering industrial implementation. The review highlights the need for optimized processing, detailed failure analysis, and a better understanding of deformation mechanisms to enable weight reduction and improved fuel efficiency.
paper_map.research_objective: To summarize the current state of knowledge on composition–processing–property relationships in lightweight Fe–Mn–Al–C steels and identify research gaps that must be addressed for their practical industrial application.
material_systems: reference materials: rolled homogeneous armor (RHA), high-strength low-alloy (HSLA) steel, titanium-based armor alloys
main_process_variables: Alloy composition (Mn, Al, C, Si, Cr, Cu, V, Nb, Mo, Ni)

key_sections.heading: 1. Introduction
key_sections.description: Background, motivation for lightweight steels, influence of stacking fault energy on deformation mechanisms (TRIP, TWIP, MBIP), and scope of the review.

key_sections.heading: 2. The Influence of Main Elements on Phase Distribution
key_sections.description: Effect of Mn, Al, and C on phase formation, and comprehensive analysis of κ-carbide characteristics, precipitation, and their impact on mechanical properties.

key_sections.heading: 3. Influence of Alloying and Microalloying on the Properties
key_sections.description: Impact of alloying elements (Si, Cr, Cu) and microalloying carbide-formers (V, Nb, Mo, Ni) on structure and properties, including combined effects.

key_sections.heading: 4. Manufacturing Process
key_sections.description: Production challenges, melt-refractory reactions, casting issues, and processing routes for Fe–Mn–Al–C steels.

key_sections.heading: 5. Prospective Research Directions
key_sections.description: Open issues for implementation: optimizing processing parameters, failure behavior, mechanical anisotropy, hydrogen embrittlement, and control of deformation-strengthening mechanisms.

key_sections.heading: 6. Conclusion
key_sections.description: Summary of the potential of lightweight TWIP steels, influence of processing on property variation, and the need for continued R&D to overcome production difficulties.
